# unison-fs

> Rust filesystem that mounts the Unison brain as a real local directory — FUSE
> on Linux, embedded NFS on macOS — with a local SQLite cache and background
> sync loop, so editors, scripts, and AI agents can read and write the brain
> like any local folder.

**Agents: start with [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md)** — it tells you how to install the
mount, authenticate with `UNISON_TOKEN`, and use the filesystem from code.

## Docs
- [README.md](README.md): install, quickstart, env vars, brain layout, commands
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): how an agent installs, authenticates, mounts, and uses unisonfs
- [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md): build, test, lint, PR checklist
- [SECURITY.md](SECURITY.md): vulnerability reporting

## Usage
- Install: `cargo install --path crates/unisonfs` or `curl -fsSL .../install.sh | bash`
- Auth: `export UNISON_TOKEN=usk_...` or `unisonfs login`
- Mount: `unisonfs mount ~/brain`
- Write: `echo "# My Note" > ~/brain/private/notes/idea.md`
- Read: `cat ~/brain/private/notes/idea.md`
- Unmount: `unisonfs unmount ~/brain`

## Examples
- [crates/unisonfs/src/cmd/](crates/unisonfs/src/cmd/): CLI subcommand implementations
- [crates/unisonfs-core/src/api/](crates/unisonfs-core/src/api/): typed Rust API client
- [crates/unisonfs-core/src/sync/](crates/unisonfs-core/src/sync/): pull + push loop implementations
